search:spring-data-jpa-redis:spring data.content:
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `mvn clean install` to build the project.
  - `./gradlew bootRun` to run the Spring Boot application.

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Spring Data JPA: 2.7.3.RELEASE
  - Spring Data Redis: 2.6.4.RELEASE

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `spring.redis.host`: Set the Redis server host.
  - `spring.redis.port`: Set the Redis server port.
  - `spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.cache.use_second_level_cache=true`: Enable Hibernate second-level cache.
  - `spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.cache.region.factory_class=org.springframework.data.redis.cache.RedisCacheRegionFactory`: Specify the cache region factory.

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `mvn clean install` to build your project.
  - `java -jar target/your-application.jar` to run the application.

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Spring Boot: 3.0.x
  - Spring Data JPA: 2.7.x
  - Redis: 6.2.x

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `spring.redis.host`: Set your Redis server address.
  - `spring.redis.port`: Set your Redis server port.
  - `spring.data.jpa.properties.hibernate.cache.use_second_level_cache`: Enable Hibernate 2nd level cache.
  - `spring.data.jpa.properties.hibernate.cache.region.factory_class`: Specify the cache region factory class (e.g., `org.springframework.orm.jpa.hibernate.SpringCacheRegionFactory`).
  - `spring.redis.password`: Set your Redis password if authentication is enabled.

- Use `@EnableCaching` annotation in your Spring Boot application.
- Configure Redis cache manager using `RedisCacheManager.builder(RedisConnectionFactory connectionFactory)`.
- Implement caching strategies like read-through, write-through, or cache-aside.
- Set TTL (Time To Live) for cache entries with `@Cacheable(value = "cacheName", key = "#id", unless = "#result == null")`.
- Use `RedisTemplate` for direct Redis operations if needed.
- Ensure consistent access to entries with TTL expiration on every read or write operation.
- Monitor and tune cache performance using Spring Boot Actuator.

- **GitHub Repository**: [fahdarhalai/sample-spring-data-jpa-redis-caching](https://github.com/fahdarhalai/sample-spring-data-jpa-redis-caching)
- **Latest Spring Boot Version**: As of my last update, the latest stable version is 2.7.x.
- **Redis Cache Configuration**:
  - Add dependency in `pom.xml`:
    ```xml
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-redis</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    ```
  - Configure Redis properties in `application.properties`:
    ```properties
    spring.redis.host=localhost
    spring.redis.port=6379
    ```
- **Spring Data JPA Caching**:
  - Enable caching in your Spring Boot application by adding the following annotation to your main class or configuration class:
    ```java
    @EnableCaching
    ```
  - Use `@Cacheable`, `@CachePut`, and `@CacheEvict` annotations on your repository methods.
- **Key Config Options**:
  - `spring.cache.type`: Set to `redis`.
  - `spring.redis.cache.time-to-live`: Specify the time-to-live for cache entries in seconds.

These steps provide a concrete guide for implementing Spring Data JPA with Redis caching, including necessary dependencies and configuration options.
